surface.name: Together AI general Quickstart, Python serverless chat route
surface.selection_basis: The general Quickstart is the docs-home getting-started route and ends with a printed MiniMax M3 response and an explicit first-request congratulations.

alternatives_considered.name: TypeScript SDK
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer language tab; Python is the first SDK shown.

alternatives_considered.name: cURL or REST
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer transport tab rather than a separate recommended surface.

alternatives_considered.name: Dedicated endpoint Quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It provisions billable dedicated hardware, while the general Quickstart uses serverless inference.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Together AI account with an official OAuth provider, buy the minimum prepaid credits, create a default-project API key, and run the Python first query.
assumed_prior_state: No Together AI account, organization, project, payment method, credits, API key, SDK, or request is assumed.
The developer has either a Google or GitHub identity, a supported payment card, and Python with pip.
No OAuth consent, account, billing action, key, package installation, or request was executed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Congrats! You just made your first request to Together AI.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The Python program sends the published MiniMax M3 chat prompt and prints the returned answer.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: After `python main.py`, the model response appears in the terminal after a few seconds.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Congrats! You just made your first request to Together AI.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The Quickstart explicitly names the first request and puts streaming variations and other product work afterward.

prerequisites.requirement: A Google or GitHub account for OAuth signup; Together does not use a traditional username and password.
A supported credit or debit card and a minimum $5 prepaid credit purchase; there is no current free trial.
A project-scoped API key copied at its one-time display.
Python and pip for the selected official SDK route.

primary_path.action: Open the general Quickstart and follow Register for an account.
primary_path.success_signal: Google and GitHub OAuth options are available as documented.

primary_path.action: Choose Google or GitHub and complete that provider's OAuth sign-in.
details: The same provider must be used for future sign-ins.
primary_path.input: Selected provider identity and its authorization
primary_path.output: A Together AI account is set up.
primary_path.success_signal: The account's organization and Default Project are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Using a different provider later produces `This email is already linked to another sign-in method.`

primary_path.action: Provide the account's organization with its included Default Project.
primary_path.output: The developer has access to the Default Project.
primary_path.success_signal: Project API key settings can be opened.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No Default Project provisioning wait or recovery is documented.

primary_path.action: Open the Default Project, then its API keys settings.
primary_path.output: The project key list and Create API Key action are visible.

primary_path.action: Select Create API Key, enter a descriptive name, optionally set expiration, and select Create.
primary_path.output: A new project-scoped key is generated.
primary_path.success_signal: The full key value is displayed once.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No key-generation failure recovery is documented.

primary_path.action: Copy the key immediately and save it securely.
primary_path.output: The developer retains the API credential.
primary_path.success_signal: The key can be exported locally.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If lost, create a new key because the value cannot be shown again.

primary_path.output: The Together SDK can read the key automatically.

primary_path.action: Open Billing settings, select Manage payment methods, add a supported card, and make it the default.
details: Prepaid and unsupported cards are rejected; a provider verification step may be required.
primary_path.output: A valid default payment method is saved.
primary_path.success_signal: The card is available for a manual credit purchase.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: For a decline, verify balance, address, expiry, activation, CVV, recurring-payment support, authorization hold, and bank prompts.

primary_path.action: In Billing settings select Add Credits, purchase at least $5, and wait for the credit balance to update.
details: Together AI is fully prepaid and does not currently offer a free trial.
primary_path.output: Purchased credits are added to the organization balance.
primary_path.success_signal: A positive purchased-credit balance is shown.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Credit purchases can take up to 15 minutes; while pending, do not submit another payment because each is a separate purchase.

primary_path.output: The official Together Python SDK is installed.
primary_path.success_signal: The Together class can be imported.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No package-install failure recovery is documented in the Quickstart.

primary_path.action: Save the published Python snippet as `main.py`.
details: It initializes Together, calls `MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M3`, asks for the top three things to do in New York, and prints the response content.
primary_path.success_signal: The file contains the documented client, model, prompt, and print call.

primary_path.input: Funded account, exported key, installed SDK, and example file
primary_path.output: Together AI processes the chat-completion request.
primary_path.success_signal: After a few seconds the model response is printed in the terminal.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the balance reaches zero, API access is suspended until credits are added.

candidate_paths.name: Google OAuth, Default Project key, prepaid card credits, and Python first request
candidate_paths.selection_basis: Google is one of the two current official signup providers; this candidate resolves the identity-provider branch while preserving the Quickstart's Python route.
candidate_paths.relationship_to_primary_path: It instantiates the common OAuth choice at step 2 as Google and otherwise follows the same Default Project, billing, credential, SDK, and query spine.

steps.action: Open the Quickstart and follow Register for an account.
Select Google and complete Google OAuth sign-in and authorization.
details: Continue using Google for later Together AI sign-ins.
steps.output: A Together AI account is set up.
steps.success_signal: The account organization and Default Project are available.
steps.failure_or_wait: A later different-provider attempt triggers the documented linked-method error.

steps.action: Provide the organization and included Default Project.
steps.success_signal: Project API key settings can be opened.

steps.action: Open the Default Project and its API keys settings.
steps.success_signal: The key form can be opened.

steps.action: Create a named project key with optional expiration.
steps.output: The full new key is displayed once.

steps.action: Copy and securely save the one-time key value.
steps.failure_or_wait: Create another key if this value is lost.

steps.action: Add a supported card in Billing settings and make it the default.
steps.output: A default payment method is saved.
steps.success_signal: The card is ready for Add Credits.
steps.failure_or_wait: Use the documented card and address checks for declines.

steps.action: Purchase at least $5 through Add Credits and wait for the balance update.
steps.output: A positive purchased-credit balance is shown.
steps.failure_or_wait: Wait up to 15 minutes if pending and do not duplicate the purchase.

steps.action: Save the published MiniMax M3 example as `main.py`.

steps.success_signal: The response is printed after a few seconds.
steps.failure_or_wait: Zero balance suspends API access until credits are added.

branches.path: Authenticate with the chosen provider and always reuse it for later sign-ins.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the external identity and consent route, then converges on the same organization and Default Project.

branches.path: Set an optional expiration date for the key.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes credential lifetime but not the first request.

branches.path: After enablement, add a US bank account while retaining a valid card and wait one to three business days for credit settlement.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds approval and settlement delay, so the selected generally available card route is faster.

branches.path: Use the corresponding Quickstart install and request tab.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes local tooling while preserving the printed-response terminal.

friction_gates.description: Google and GitHub are peer OAuth signup providers, and the choice changes the identity and consent route.
The project key is displayed only once and must be saved immediately.
Platform access requires a supported card; bank or provider verification can add a confirmation step.
There is no free trial and at least $5 of prepaid credits is required before API calls.
A credit purchase can remain pending for up to 15 minutes before the balance updates.

time_to_first_success.value: The current Quickstart says the first request takes a few minutes and the response prints after a few seconds; these are vendor claims, not measured end-to-end timings.

sources.title: Projects
evidence_supported: Every organization includes a Default Project

uncertainties.question: Which OAuth provider should a new developer use?
uncertainties.impact: The choice changes external identity, consent, and future sign-in requirements, so the record remains needs-human-judgment despite the complete Google candidate.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Official docs present Google and GitHub as peers without a default.

uncertainties.question: What exact Google or GitHub authorization screens and scopes appear?
uncertainties.impact: The provider-managed sub-screen count cannot be stated.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Together documents OAuth provider choices and convergence but does not reproduce the providers' current consent screens.
